long-title: Children and young people leaving school with low health literacy

sections.long-title: What is the level being targeted?

content.choice-subtitle: Things that happen between individuals, like one-to-one support or care

content.long-title: Who should be involved?
content: Parents
'Hidden' caregivers (such as relatives)
Children

content.long-title: What are the causes of the problem?
content: Carers that no-one knows about (e.g. relatives)
Children and young people not feeling part of society

content.long-title: What actions are involved in doing something about this?
content: Reading groups which focus on improving literacy skills for children and young people

content.long-title: How will we deliver these actions?
content: Children and young people’s reading groups

content.long-title: What do we expect to come out of these actions?
content: Skills to read for health
Skills to count for health
Skills to manage money
Children and young people having better health and well-being
Children and young people leading healthier lives
Healthier social interactions

content.long-title: How will we know things have changed?
content: Reading, counting and speaking skills for health will be better

content.long-title: What will have happened in 3 years?
content: Improved health literacy in children and young people
Healthier lifestyle – exercise, diet, smoking, alcohol
Increased health and well-being
Healthier social interactions

content.choice-subtitle: Things that happen in organisations or communities, like teamwork or service coordination
content.long-title: What would help?
Who should be involved?
content: Teachers
Schools
Libraries
Community Centres
Places of Worship
Nurseries

content.long-title: What are the causes of the problem?
content: Lack of teaching about health and illness
Teaching curriculum that is too full

content.long-title: What would help to solve the problem?
content: Health literacy in teacher training
Health literacy in social work training
Teaching health literacy in schools

content.long-title: What actions are involved in doing something about this?
content: Staff training courses
Frequency of staff training
Engaging with senior managers in schools
Mapping out children and young people’s teaching organisations

content.long-title: How will we deliver these actions?
content: Training courses online and/or face-to-face

content.long-title: What do we expect to come out of these actions?
content: Number of staff trained
Map and monitor health literacy teaching in schools

content.long-title: How will we know things have changed?
content: More staff will have been trained
More action plans will be in place in places where children and young people learn

content.long-title: What will have changed in 1 year?
content: Mapping out of institutions and places to be trained
Training courses planned

content.long-title: What will have happened in 3 years?
content: Staff trained in health literacy

content.choice-subtitle: Big system-wide things, like national policies or data systems
content.long-title: What would help?
Who should be involved?
content: Local councils
Organisations who write the national curriculum

content.long-title: What are the causes of the problem?
content: Poverty
The education system is not working properly

content.long-title: What would help to solve the problem?
content: Policies that support health literacy being part of professional training courses
Changing the national curriculum to include more health literacy

content.long-title: What actions are involved in doing something about this?
content: Engaging with local councils (to influence policy, funding, and strategic planning)

content.long-title: What do we expect to come out of these actions?
content: Understanding what is needed to improve health literacy in children

content.long-title: What will have changed in 1 year?
content: Better understanding what is needed to improve health literacy in children

content.long-title: What will have happened in 3 years?
content: National curriculum changes to include health literacy
Public health campaigns targeting youth health literacy
Policy shifts supporting health education in schools

sections.long-title: What are the current activities in this area?

content.long-title: Project one!
Project two!
